Scythe Blades (BB).description: Zwei in der Decke verborgene, einer 15 Fuß/4,50 m langen Linie folgende Klingen, verbunden mit einem Stolperdraht.

action->Falling Scythes.name: Herabsausende Sensenklingen
action->Falling Scythes.description: Wird der Stolperdraht durchtrennt oder bewegt, schwingen beide Klingen herab; jede Klinge greift alle Kreaturen auf ihrer Linie an. Die Falle führt einen Nahkampfangriff mit jeder Klinge mit +17 auf jeden Angriffswurf aus. Diese Angriffen unterliegen keinem Malus für Mehrfachangriffe. Bei einem Treffer verursacht eine Klinge 2W12+4 Hiebschaden. Bei einem Kritischen Treffer wird der Schaden verdoppelt, zuzüglich 6 Punkte Zusatzschaden.

Sea Hag (BB).name: Sea Hag (BB)
Sea Hag (BB).publicNotes: Hags are malevolent predators who use magic and manipulation to lure children and young adults into their clutches. They seek out targets who are unhappy, innocent, or otherwise vulnerable, preying on their weaknesses before snatching them up. A sea hag has the upper half of a humanoid and the lower half of an octopus, with translucent skin and glowing lights visible beneath their flesh.
Sea Hag (BB).saveDetails: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Dread Gaze.name: Dread Gaze
action->Dread Gaze.description:
The hag gazes upon a creature, afflicting it with a gnawing sense of impending doom, with a result depending on its @Check[will|dc:20|options:inflicts:frightened,inflicts:slowed] save. The target doesn't need to be able to see the sea hag.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The creature is frightened 1 and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.xYTAsEpcJE1Ccni3]{Slowed 1} for 1 round.

Critical Failure As failure, but the creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2} and slowed 1 for 1 minute.

Severed Head (BB).name: Severed Head (BB)
Severed Head (BB).publicNotes: Severed heads are the reanimated heads of decapitation victims. These mindless undead fly through the air or roll around to attack their prey. In addition to attacking the living like other undead, severed heads can sometimes be drawn to things important to them in life. Scholars argue that this is likely due to particularly powerful thoughts that can break through momentarily in what's left of a severed head's mind. Unfortunately, their mindless state prevents them from recognizing their goal, and they remain violent even after encountering what they seek.

action->Gnash.name: Gnash
action->Gnash.description: When the severed head succeeds at its jaws Strike, it can use this action immediately after to make a second jaws Strike at the same target as it violently shakes itself, trying to rip away a mouthful of flesh. On a success, the target takes an additional 1d4 slashing damage and 1 persistent bleed damage.

Sewer Ooze (BB).name: Kanalschlick (EB)
Sewer Ooze (BB).publicNotes: Diese gestaltlosen Massen aus Abwasser, Fäkalien und anderen Abfällen bewegen sich durch die großen und kleinen verdreckten Abflusskanäle unter den Städten. Manche erreichen gewaltige Größen und haben einen unersättlichen Appetit.

action->Motion Sense 60 feet.name: Bewegungsgespür 60 Fuß/18 m
action->Motion Sense 60 feet.description: Ein Kanalschlick kann durch Vibrationen und Luftbewegungen in der Nähe Bewegungen wahrnehmen; dies erlaubt ihm zu kämpfen, als könnte er sehen. Auf Sicht basierende Effekte funktionieren aber nicht gegen ihn.

action->Filth Wave.name: Schmutzwelle
action->Filth Wave.description:
Der Kanalschlick entfesselt eine Welle aus Schmutz, die alle Kreaturen innerhalb von @Template[emanation|distance:20]{20 Fuß/6 m} Entfernung zu ihm bedeckt. Jede Kreatur im Wirkungsbereich muss einen @Check[reflex|dc:17|options:area-effect,damaging-effect]{Reflexwurf} ablegen; bei Misslingen erleidet sie @Damage[1d4[acid]|options:area-damage]{1W4 Punkte Säureschaden} und erhält für 1 Minute einen Malus von -10 Fuß/3 m auf alle Bewegungsraten (im Falle eines Kritischen Fehlschlages stürzt sie zudem und erhält den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Liegend}). Eine Kreatur kann eine Handlung zum Interagieren aufwenden, um jemanden zu säubern; dies reduziert den Malus auf die Bewegungsrate pro aufgewandte Aktion um 5 Fuß/1,50 m. Der Schlick kann diese Fähigkeit dann 1 Minute lang nicht anwenden.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.Qomp2EujVCbzJb4X]{Effekt: Schmutzwelle}

Shadow (BB).name: Schatten (EB)
Shadow (BB).publicNotes: Untote Schatten nähren sich von jenen, die sich zu weit vom Licht entfernen.

action->Incorporeal.name: Körperlos
action->Incorporeal.description: Ein Schatten besitzt keinen Körper und kann daher feste Gegenstände durchqueren. Er kann seinen Zug aber nicht in einem Gegenstand beenden. Die meisten Handlungen, welche auf Stärke basieren und einen Körper benötigen, wie z.B. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Ergreifen} und @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Fortstoßen}, funktionieren nicht gegen einen Schatten und können auch nicht von Schatten eingesetzt werden.

action->Light Vulnerability.name: Empfindlichkeit gegen Licht
action->Light Vulnerability.description: Angriffe gegen den Schatten werden als magisch behandelt, sofern sich der Schatten oder die genutzte Waffe im Licht befindet.

action->Slink in Shadows.name: In Schatten eintauchen
action->Slink in Shadows.description: Der Schatten kann sich im Schatten einer Kreatur oder Gegenstandes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.XMcnh4cSI32tljXa]{Verstecken} oder dort sein @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.VMozDqMMuK5kpoX4]{Schleichen} beenden.

action->Steal Shadow.name: Schatten stehlen
action->Steal Shadow.description: If the shadow hits a living creature with a Strike on its previous action, it can use this action to pull at the target's shadow, imposing a –1 status penalty to Athletics and to attack rolls and damage rolls that use Strength (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1}). The penalty increases each time a shadow uses this ability to a maximum of –4. The penalty decreases by 1 every hour.

Shadow Bat.name: Shadow Bat
Shadow Bat.publicNotes: The shadowy magical energies related to some wicked deities, evil forces, or the shadowy plane of existence called the Netherworld sometimes manifest in the form of corrupted living creatures. The shadow bat has the physique of a giant bat made of shadows with the magical ability to create powerful gusts of wind.

action->Echolocation.name: Echolocation
action->Echolocation.description: The bat can "see" using its hearing at the listed range, allowing it to function just as well in darkness.

action->Screeching Gust.name: Screeching Gust
action->Screeching Gust.description:
The shadow bat flaps its wings and lets out a mighty screech, creating a cone of tearing winds and piercing sound. All creatures in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] take @Damage[3d6[sonic]|options:area-damage] damage, with a @Check[reflex|dc:16|basic|options:area-effect,forced-movement] saving throw. Creatures that fail are pushed backwards 10 feet by the wind.

Using its wings in this way exhausts the shadow bat, causing it to fall harmlessly to the ground immediately after using this ability and preventing it from taking flight or using Screeching Gust again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Screeching Gust]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Clawed Feet.name: Clawed Feet
action->Clawed Feet.description: If the gargoyle is flying and a creature moves into an adjacent square below them, the gargoyle can use their reaction to make a claw Strike against that creature.

Shadow Priest.name: Shadow Priest
Shadow Priest.publicNotes: Sometimes, followers of unholy deities will transform into shadows upon their death, a gift from their wicked patrons.

action->Incorporeal.name: Incorporeal
action->Incorporeal.description: The shadow priest has no body and can pass through physical objects, though they can't end their turn inside one. Most checks that use Strength and require a body, such as @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.PMbdMWc2QroouFGD]{Grapple} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.7blmbDrQFNfdT731]{Shove}, don't work against the shadow priest, and the shadow priest can't use them.

action->Light Vulnerability.name: Light Vulnerability
action->Light Vulnerability.description: Attacks against the shadow priest are treated as magical if the shadow priest or the weapon being used against them is in light.

action->Shadow Veil.name: Shadow Veil
action->Shadow Veil.description: If the shadow priest is targeted by a melee attack, they can use their reaction to gain a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack.

Shadow Twigjack.name: Shadow Twigjack
Shadow Twigjack.publicNotes:
"True" twigjacks, as they consider themselves, are found in wild forests alongside the fey they idolize.

Maladjusted forest denizens, twigjacks form from the cruel and prankish combination of fey and the very woods in which they reside. A twigjack's body is made up of prickly brambles woven with vines. Shaggy, mossy growth, not unlike hair, tops a twigjack's head. Its mouth is just a canyon of splintered and broken sticks bisecting its face. Leaves and sprigs of new growth randomly sprout from the creature's body. Many dense forests on Golarion have at least a handful of twigjacks living in the undergrowth.

While truculent and violent, twigjacks care deeply for what they consider to be their forests. These creatures harass outsiders who delve deep into their wooded domains, forcing back even the most determined explorers, foresters, and travelers, especially when those intruders cut roads through the forest. However, they are not terribly territorial when it comes to other forest creatures. When sylvan creatures, especially fey, rally against an outside threat, twigjacks in the area eagerly arrive to fight, even if they were not invited.

action->Bramble Jump.name: Bramble Jump
action->Bramble Jump.description: When the twigjack is in undergrowth, it can instantly teleport to a square of undergrowth within 60 feet. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

action->Splinter Spray.name: Splinter Spray
action->Splinter Spray.description: The twigjack sprays a barrage of splinters and brambles from its body that deals @Damage[3d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:18|basic|options:area-effect] save. The twigjack can't use Splinter Spray again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Splinter Spray]]{1d4 rounds}.

Shadow Walls.name: Shadow Walls
Shadow Walls.disable: @Check[arcana|dc:20], @Check[nature|dc:20], @Check[occultism|dc:20], or @Check[religion|dc:20] on each of the shadow walls (disabling three of the four walls ends the hazard), or activating the orrery with the unlit star as noted in "The Orrery of Light."
Shadow Walls.routine:
Each turn, the walls use 1 action. Roll a [[/gmr 1d10 #Routine Result]]{d10} and look up the result on the following list to determine which action the walls uses that round.

1–4 Single Target The walls lash out at a random hero in the room. Roll a [[/gmr 1d4 #Random Hero]]{d4} and count to the number you rolled, starting with the player on your left, to determine which hero gets attacked. The shadow lash is a ranged Strike with +10 to hit. On a hit, it deals @Damage[2d6[void]] damage (doubled on a critical hit as normal).

5–7 Wall Barrage The shadow arms lunge out from their respective wall, assaulting any one near an active wall. This hits any creature within 10 feet of an active wall (within 10 feet of a shadow hand on the map), dealing @Damage[2d6[void]|options:area-damage] damage. Each hero must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15|basic|options:area-effect] save, taking no damage on a critical success, half damage on a success, full damage on a failure, and double damage on a critical failure.

8–9 Split Assault The shadows lash out against one half of the room. Roll [[/gmr 1d20 #Even Western Half, Odd Eastern Half]]{any die}. If the result is even, they assault the western half; otherwise, they assault the eastern half. This deals @Damage[2d6[void]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature in that half of the room, and each hero must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:15|basic|options:area-effect] save.

10 Overwhelming Assault Like the split assault attack, but it targets everyone in the room.

strike-ranged->Shadow Lash.name: Shadow Lash
strike-ranged->Shadow Lash.description: Note: This Strike can only be used with the Single Target result of the Shadow Walls' routine.

Shulzara.name: Shulzara
Shulzara.publicNotes: Hags are malevolent predators who use magic and manipulation to lure children and young adults into their clutches. They seek out targets who are unhappy, innocent, or otherwise vulnerable, preying on their weaknesses before snatching them up. A sea hag has the upper half of a humanoid and the lower half of an octopus, with translucent skin and glowing lights visible beneath their flesh.
Shulzara.saveDetails: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

spell->Fear.name: Fear
spell->Fear.description:
You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round.

Heightened (3rd) You can target up to five creatures.

spell->Frostbite.name: Frostbite
spell->Frostbite.description:
An orb of biting cold coalesces around your target, freezing its body. The target takes 2d4 cold damage with a basic Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the target also gains weakness 1 to bludgeoning until the start of your next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.DdqWMj7cuf4S1bgr]{Spell Effect: Frostbite}

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4 and the weakness on a critical failure increases by 1.

spell->Hydraulic Push.name: Hydraulic Push
spell->Hydraulic Push.description:
You call forth a powerful blast of pressurized water that bludgeons the target and knocks it back. Make a ranged spell attack roll.

Critical Success The target takes 6d6 bludgeoning damage and is knocked back 10 feet.

Success The target takes 3d6 bludgeoning damage and is knocked back 5 feet.

Heightened (+1) The bludgeoning damage increases by 2d6.

spell->Light.name: Light
spell->Light.description:
You create an orb of light that sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet) in a color you choose. If you create the light in the same space as a willing creature, you can attach the light to the creature, causing it to float near that creature as it moves. You can Sustain the spell to move the light up to 60 feet; you can attach or detach it from a creature as part of this movement.

You can Dismiss the spell. If you Cast the Spell while you already have four light spells active, you must choose one of the existing spells to end.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.cVVZXNbV0nElVOPZ]{Spell Effect: Light}

Heightened (4th) The orb sheds light in a 60-foot radius (and dim light for the next 60 feet).

spell->Message.name: Message
spell->Message.description:
You mouth words quietly, but instead of coming out of your mouth, they're transferred directly to the ears of the target. While others can't hear your words any better than if you normally mouthed them, the target can hear your words as if they were standing next to you. The target can give a brief response as a reaction, or as a free action on their next turn if they wish, but they must be able to see you and be within range to do so. If they respond, their response is delivered directly to your ear, just like the original message.

Heightened (3rd) The spell's range increases to 500 feet.

spell->Mud Pit.name: Mud Pit
spell->Mud Pit.description: Thick, clinging mud covers the ground, 1 foot deep. The mud is difficult terrain.

spell->Shield.name: Shield
spell->Shield.description:
You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use.

While the spell is in effect, you can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} reaction with your magic shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.Jemq5UknGdMO7b73]{Spell Effect: Shield}

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.QF6RDlCoTvkVHRo4]{Effect: Shield Immunity}

Heightened (+2) The shield's Hardness increases by 5.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Slithering Steps.name: Slithering Steps
action->Slithering Steps.description: Shulzara is not affected by difficult terrain due to water.

Skeletal Giant (BB).name: Skelettriese (EB)
Skeletal Giant (BB).publicNotes: Die reanimierten Knochen von Riesen geben exzellente nekromantische Diener ab - dies schließt auch die Gebeine von stierköpfigen Minotauren ein. Geistlose Skelette nutzen immer gleiche Taktiken, daher stellen größere und widerstandsfähige Skelette im Grunde die einzige Möglichkeit dar, wie Nekromanten an mächtigere Diener gelangen können.

action->Broad Swipe.name: Durchschlagender Hieb
action->Broad Swipe.description: Der Riese führt mit seiner Zweihandaxt jeweils einen Angriff gegen zwei angrenzende Gegner aus, die sich beide in seinem Angriffsradius befinden müssen. Der Malus für Mehrfachangriffe steigt erst, nachdem beide Angriffe ausgeführt wurden.

action->Terrifying Charge.name: Furchteinflößender Sturmangriff
action->Terrifying Charge.description:
Der Riese nutzt Laufen und führt einen Hörnerangriff mit einem Situationsbonus von +4 auf den Schaden aus.

Trifft er, legt der Riese sodann einen Fertigkeitswurf zum [[/act demoralize]]{Demoralisieren} des Ziels ab.

Skeleton Guard (BB).name: Skelettwächter (EB)
Skeleton Guard (BB).publicNotes:
Skelette bestehen aus Knochen, die von finsterer Nekromantie zusammengehalten werden. Sie zählen zu den weitverbreitetsten Arten von Untoten, welche alte Gewölbe und vergessene Friedhöfe heimsuchen. Viele böse Intriganten umgeben sich mit ewig wachsamen Untoten und müssen sich so nicht um die Versorgung und Hege und Pflege lebender Wächter kümmern.

Einfache Skelettwächter zählen zu den häufigsten Skelettdienern. Diese Skelette sind geistlos und denken nicht, stattdessen erfüllen sie wie programmiert ihre einfachen Pflichten und greifen Eindringlinge an. Verteidigt eine Gruppe aus mehreren Skeletten denselben Ort, sind in der Regel Nahkämpfer und Bogenschützen dabei, welche aus der Ferne Pfeile verschießen.

Slamming Door (BB).name: Herabfallender Steinblock (EB)
Slamming Door (BB).description: Druckplatten im Boden sind mit einem in der Decke verborgenen Steinblock verbunden.
Slamming Door (BB).disable: @Check[thievery|dc:15|traits:hazard|name:Druckplatten ausschalten]{Diebeskunst} auf die Druckplatten, ehe der Block herabstürzt

action->Slam Shut.name: Steinblock
action->Slam Shut.description: When pressure is placed on a floor tile, the door falls, closing off the hallway. The stone slab deals @Damage[3d8[bludgeoning]] damage to anyone beneath or adjacent to the slab when it drops and pushes them out of its space in a random direction. A creature that succeeds at a @Check[reflex|dc:17|options:damaging-effect] save takes no damage and rolls out of the way in a random direction. On a critical success, they roll out of the way and can choose which direction. Lifting the fallen slab requires a successful @Check[athletics|dc:25] check. Hitting the floor panels triggers the trap. The slab uses the same AC and saves as the trap, but it has Hardness 12, HP 48 (BT 24).

Sod Hound (BB).name: Erdhund (EB)
Sod Hound (BB).publicNotes:
Erdelementare wie der Erdhund geben exzellente Leibwächter für Höhlenforscher ab und sind die idealen Beschützer wichtiger unterirdischer Stätten.

Erdhunde sind moosbewachsene Hunde aus Erde und Kieseln. Sie können sich mit anderen Erdhunden zusammentun und als Rudel unterwegs sein.

action->Crystal Sense 60 feet.name: Kristallgespür 60 Fuß/18 m
action->Crystal Sense 60 feet.description: Ein Erdhund kann Kristalle und Edelsteine innerhalb von 60 Fuß/18 m wahrnehmen.

action->Earth Glide.name: Durch Erde gleiten
action->Earth Glide.description: Der Erdhund kann sich durch jede Art von Erde graben, Felsen eingeschlossen. In diesem Fall bewegt er sich um bis zu 20 Fuß/6 m weit und hinterlässt weder einen Tunnel noch andere Spuren seiner Passage.

action->Knockdown.description: Gelingt dem Erdhund ein Bissangriff, kann er sofort diese Aktion nutzen und versuchen das Ziel @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.ge56Lu1xXVFYUnLP]{Zu Fall zu bringen}. Dieser Versuch unterliegt nicht dem Malus für Mehrfachangriffe und wirkt sich auch nicht auf diesen aus.

Spear Launcher (BB).name: Speerwerfer (EB)
Spear Launcher (BB).description: In der Wand befindet sich ein mit einem Speer geladener Federmechanismus, der mit einer Bodenfliese in einem 5 Fuß/1,50 m x 5 Fuß/1,50 m-Feld verbunden ist.

action->Spear.name: Speer
action->Spear.description: Wird auf die Bodenfliese Druck ausgeübt, verschießt die Falle einen Speer und führt mit ihm einen Speer-Fernkampfangriff aus.

action->Motion Sense.name: Motion Sense
action->Motion Sense.description: A string slime can feel nearby motion through vibration and air movement.

action->Split.name: Split
action->Split.description: Whenever a string slime would take slashing damage (if it weren't immune) and has at least 10 Hit Points, it splits into two identical slimes with half the original's Hit Points. One string slime is in the same space as the original, and the other appears in an adjacent unoccupied space. If no adjacent space is unoccupied, move smaller creatures and objects out of the way to make a space or the split is canceled at the GM's discretion.

action->Tag Team.name: Tag Team
action->Tag Team.description: The slime arcs acidic protoplasm to another string slime within 30 feet along a straight line. Each creature in that line takes @Damage[3d6[acid]] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:16|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:prone] save. A creature that fails its save is also knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}.

Twigjack (BB).name: Twigjack (BB)
Twigjack (BB).publicNotes:
"True" twigjacks, as they consider themselves, are found in wild forests alongside the fey they idolize.

Maladjusted forest denizens, twigjacks form from the cruel and prankish combination of fey and the very woods in which they reside. A twigjack's body is made up of prickly brambles woven with vines. Shaggy, mossy growth, not unlike hair, tops a twigjack's head. Its mouth is just a canyon of splintered and broken sticks bisecting its face. Leaves and sprigs of new growth randomly sprout from the creature's body. Many dense forests on Golarion have at least a handful of twigjacks living in the undergrowth.

While truculent and violent, twigjacks care deeply for what they consider to be their forests. These creatures harass outsiders who delve deep into their wooded domains, forcing back even the most determined explorers, foresters, and travelers, especially when those intruders cut roads through the forest. However, they are not terribly territorial when it comes to other forest creatures. When sylvan creatures, especially fey, rally against an outside threat, twigjacks in the area eagerly arrive to fight, even if they were not invited.

action->Bramble Jump.name: Bramble Jump
action->Bramble Jump.description: When the twigjack is in undergrowth, it can instantly teleport to a square of undergrowth within 60 feet. This movement doesn't trigger reactions.

action->Splinter Spray.name: Splinter Spray
action->Splinter Spray.description: The twigjack sprays a barrage of splinters and brambles from its body that deals @Damage[4d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:20|basic|options:area-effect] save. The twigjack can't use Splinter Spray again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Splinter Spray]]{1d4 rounds}.

Umbral Gnome Rockwarden (BB).name: Umbral Gnome Rockwarden (BB)
Umbral Gnome Rockwarden (BB).publicNotes:
A notable subgroup of gnomes called umbral gnomes typically have gray or brown skin with a stony texture, and thin, pale hair or bald pates. Umbral gnomes are most numerous in the Darklands, where they go by the name drathnelar. While they are not as sinister or ruthless as other Darklands denizens, those who seek their aid should remember that they don't welcome strangers with open arms.

Umbral gnome rockwardens follow druidic teachings and commune with the natural elemental influences and denizens of the Darklands.

spell->Blindness.name: Blindness
spell->Blindness.description:
You blind the target. The effect is determined by the target's Fortitude save. The target then becomes temporarily immune for 1 minute.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} until its next turn begins.

Failure The target is Blinded for 1 minute.

Critical Failure The target is Blinded permanently.

spell->One with Stone.name: One with Stone
spell->One with Stone.description:
You can either transform into a stone or merge with stone. While transformed, you can't move or affect anything outside the stone, but you can cast spells as long as they don't require line of effect beyond the stone. You can Dismiss this spell.

  • Merge with Stone The spell's duration is 10 minutes. While casting the spell, you must touch a stone with enough volume to fit you and your possessions or the spell is disrupted. While merged, you can hear, but not see, what's going on outside the stone. If the stone takes damage while you're inside it, you're expelled from the stone and take 10d6 damage. Magic passage expels you without dealing damage. The spell ends if you're ever outside the stone.
  • Turn into a Stone The spell's duration is 8 hours. You become a Large stone. Perception checks don't reveal your true nature, but a successful Nature or Survival check against your spell DC reveals that you appear to be a stone that is strangely new to the area. While in this form, you can observe everything around you, using your normal senses. As a stone, your AC is 23, and only status bonuses, status penalties, circumstance bonuses, and circumstance penalties affect you. Any successes and critical successes you roll on Reflex saves are failures

spell->Acid Grip.name: Acid Grip
spell->Acid Grip.description:
An ephemeral, taloned hand grips the target, burning it with magical acid. The target takes 2d8 acid damage plus @Damage[floor(@item.level/2)d6[persistent,acid]] damage depending on its Reflex save. A creature taking persistent damage from this spell takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.6TGcfVyzzVHEo7ke]{Spell Effect: Acid Grip}

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and no persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 5 feet in a direction of your choice.

Failure The creature takes full damage and persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 10 feet in a direction of your choice.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and full persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 20 feet in a direction of your choice.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

spell->Blazing Bolt.name: Blazing Bolt
spell->Blazing Bolt.description:
You fire a ray of heat and flame. Make a spell attack roll against a single creature. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 fire damage, and on a critical hit, the target takes double damage.

For each additional action you use when Casting the Spell, you can fire an additional ray at a different target, to a maximum of three rays targeting three different targets for 3 actions. These attacks each increase your multiple attack penalty, but you don't increase your multiple attack penalty until after you make all the spell attack rolls for blazing bolt. If you spend 2 or more actions Casting the Spell, the damage increases to 4d6 fire damage on a hit, and it still deals double damage on a critical hit.

Heightened (+1) The damage to each target increases by 1d6 for the 1-action version, or by 2d6 for the 2-action and 3-action versions.

spell->Resist Energy.name: Resist Energy
spell->Resist Energy.description:
A shield of elemental energy protects a creature against one type of energy damage. Choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage. The target and its gear gain resistance 5 against the damage type you chose.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.con2Hzt47JjpuUej]{Spell Effect: Resist Energy}

Heightened (4th) The resistance increases to 10, and you can target up to two creatures.

Heightened (7th) The resistance increases to 15, and you can target up to five creatures.

spell->Caustic Blast.name: Caustic Blast
spell->Caustic Blast.description:
You fling a large glob of acid that immediately detonates, spraying nearby creatures. Creatures in the area take 1d8 acid damage with a basic Reflex save; on a critical failure, the creature also takes @Damage[(1+floor((@item.rank -1)/2))[persistent,acid]] damage.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 1d8, and the persistent damage on a critical failure increases by 1.

spell->Detect Magic.name: Detect Magic
spell->Detect Magic.description:
You send out a pulse that registers the presence of magic. You receive no information beyond the presence or absence of magic. You can choose to ignore magic you're fully aware of, such as the magic items and ongoing spells of you and your allies. You detect illusion magic only if that magic's effect has a lower rank than the rank of your detect magic spell. However, items that have an illusion aura but aren't deceptive in appearance (such as an invisibility potion) typically are detected normally.

Heightened (3rd) You learn the rank or level of the most powerful magical effect the spell detects, as determined by the GM.

Heightened (4th) As 3rd rank, but you also pinpoint the source of the highest-rank magic. Like for an imprecise sense, you don't learn the exact location, but can narrow down the source to within a 5-foot cube (or the nearest if larger than that).

spell->Fear.name: Fear
spell->Fear.description:
You plant fear in the target; it must attempt a Will save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected.

Success The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.sDPxOjQ9kx2RZE8D]{Fleeing} for 1 round.

Heightened (3rd) You can target up to five creatures.

spell->Frostbite.name: Frostbite
spell->Frostbite.description:
An orb of biting cold coalesces around your target, freezing its body. The target takes 2d4 cold damage with a basic Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the target also gains weakness 1 to bludgeoning until the start of your next turn.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.DdqWMj7cuf4S1bgr]{Spell Effect: Frostbite}

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d4 and the weakness on a critical failure increases by 1.

spell->Message.name: Message
spell->Message.description:
You mouth words quietly, but instead of coming out of your mouth, they're transferred directly to the ears of the target. While others can't hear your words any better than if you normally mouthed them, the target can hear your words as if they were standing next to you. The target can give a brief response as a reaction, or as a free action on their next turn if they wish, but they must be able to see you and be within range to do so. If they respond, their response is delivered directly to your ear, just like the original message.

Heightened (3rd) The spell's range increases to 500 feet.

spell->Mud Pit.name: Mud Pit
spell->Mud Pit.description: Thick, clinging mud covers the ground, 1 foot deep. The mud is difficult terrain.

spell->Shield.name: Shield
spell->Shield.description:
You raise a magical shield of force. This counts as using the Raise a Shield action, giving you a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn, but it doesn't require a hand to use.

While the spell is in effect, you can use the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.jM72TjJ965jocBV8]{Shield Block} reaction with your magic shield. The shield has Hardness 5. You can use the spell's reaction to reduce damage from any spell or magical effect, even if it doesn't deal physical damage. After you use Shield Block, the spell ends and you can't cast it again for 10 minutes.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.Jemq5UknGdMO7b73]{Spell Effect: Shield}

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.QF6RDlCoTvkVHRo4]{Effect: Shield Immunity}

Heightened (+2) The shield's Hardness increases by 5.

spell->Thunderstrike.name: Thunderstrike
spell->Thunderstrike.description:
You call down a tendril of lightning that cracks with thunder, dealing 1d12 electricity damage and 1d4 sonic damage to the target with a basic Reflex save. A target wearing metal armor or made of metal takes a –1 circumstance bonus to its save, and if damaged by the spell is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for 1 round.

Heightened (+1) The damage increases by 1d12 electricity and 1d4 sonic.

weapon->Sling.name: Sling
weapon->Sling.description: Little more than a leather cup attached to a pair of straps, a sling can be used to fling smooth stones or sling bullets at a range.

weapon->Scythe.name: Scythe
weapon->Scythe.description: Derived from a farming tool used to mow down long grains and cereals, this weapon has a long wooden shaft with protruding handles, capped with a curved blade set at a right angle.

armor->Hide Armor.name: Hide Armor
armor->Hide Armor.description: A mix of furs, sturdy hide, and sometimes molded boiled leather, this armor provides protection due to its layers of leather, though its bulkiness slows the wearer down and decreases mobility.

equipment->Primal Symbol.name: Primal Symbol
equipment->Primal Symbol.description: Primal spellcasters, especially druids, often wear adornments of natural materials to symbolize their connection to nature, such as rings of woven plants, tokens made from animal parts, or other symbols related to a druidic order or nature philosophy.

ammo->Sling Bullets.name: Sling Bullets
ammo->Sling Bullets.description: These are small metal balls, typically either iron or lead, designed to be used as ammunition in slings.

Umbral Gnome Scout (BB).name: Umbral Gnome Scout (BB)
Umbral Gnome Scout (BB).publicNotes: Umbral gnome scouts patrol the tunnels that lead into their settlements. They make use of the darkness of these tunnels to hide from any would-be trespassers. While umbral gnome scouts prefer to use their stealth for reconnaissance, they aren't afraid to strike from the shadows to deal with threats. They are adept at moving between areas of darkness for hit-and-run tactics.

spell->Ill Omen.name: Ill Omen
spell->Ill Omen.description:
The target is struck with misfortune, which throws it off balance. The target must attempt a Will save.

Success The target is unaffected.

Failure The first time during the duration that the target attempts an attack roll or skill check, it must roll twice and use the worse result.

Critical Failure Every time during the duration that the target attempts an attack roll or skill check, it must roll twice and use the worse result.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.AmsVO5Q6078mEvNt]{Spell Effect: Ill Omen}

weapon->Sling.name: Sling
weapon->Sling.description: Little more than a leather cup attached to a pair of straps, a sling can be used to fling smooth stones or sling bullets at a range.

weapon->Sickle.name: Sickle
weapon->Sickle.description: Originally a farming tool used for reaping grain, this one-handed weapon has a short wooden handle ending in a curved blade, sometimes sharpened on both sides.

ammo->Sling Bullets.name: Sling Bullets
ammo->Sling Bullets.description: These are small metal balls, typically either iron or lead, designed to be used as ammunition in slings.

action->Hidden Movement.name: Hidden Movement
action->Hidden Movement.description: If the umbral gnome scout starts their turn @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VRSef5y1LmL2Hkjf]{Undetected} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.iU0fEDdBp3rXpTMC]{Hidden} to a creature, that creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Off-Guard} against the umbral gnome scout's attacks until the end of the turn.

Umbral Gnome Warrior (BB).name: Umbral Gnome Warrior (BB)
Umbral Gnome Warrior (BB).publicNotes: Umbral gnome warriors are quick to charge into battle, but focus on defending their kin and their homes over more aggressive tactics when a choice is available. These warriors tend to work in groups and use their shields to intercept attacks.

spell->Illusory Disguise.name: Illusory Disguise
spell->Illusory Disguise.description:
You create an illusion that causes the target to appear as another creature of the same body shape, and with roughly similar height (within 6 inches) and weight (within 50 pounds). The disguise is typically good enough to hide their identity, but not to impersonate a specific individual. The spell changes their appearance and voice, but not mannerisms. You can change the appearance of its clothing and worn items, such as making its armor look like a dress. Held items are unaffected, and any worn item removed from the creature returns to its true appearance.

Casting illusory disguise counts as setting up a disguise for the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.AJstokjdG6iDjVjE]{Impersonate} use of Deception; it ignores any circumstance penalties the target might take for disguising itself as a dissimilar creature, gives a +4 status bonus to Deception checks to prevent others from seeing through the disguise, and lets the target add its level to such Deception checks even if untrained. You can Dismiss this spell.

Heightened (3rd) The target can appear as any creature of the same size, even a specific individual. You must have seen an individual to replicate its appearance, and must have heard its voice to replicate its voice.

Heightened (4th) You can target up to 10 willing creatures. If you target multiple creatures, you can choose a different disguise for each target, but none can impersonate a specific individual. You can Dismiss each disguise individually or all collectively.

Heightened (7th) As 4th, but you can choose disguises that impersonate specific individuals. You must have seen an individual to replicate its appearance, and must have heard its voice to replicate its voice.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.3Ktyd5F9lOPo4myk]{Spell Effect: Illusory Disguise}

weapon->Heavy Crossbow.name: Heavy Crossbow
weapon->Heavy Crossbow.description: This large crossbow is harder to load and more substantial than a regular crossbow, but it packs a greater punch.

weapon->Spear.name: Spear
weapon->Spear.description: A long metal shaft ending with a metal spike, a spear can be used one-handed as a melee weapon and can be thrown.

shield->Steel Shield.name: Steel Shield
shield->Steel Shield.description:
Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

Hardness HP BT
5 20 10

armor->Studded Leather Armor.name: Studded Leather Armor
armor->Studded Leather Armor.description: This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.

ammo->Bolts.name: Bolts
ammo->Bolts.description: Shorter than traditional arrows but similar in construction, bolts are the ammunition used by crossbows.

action->Raise a Shield.name: Raise a Shield
action->Raise a Shield.description: The umbral gnome warrior lifts up their shield to get a +2 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of their next turn.

action->Shield Block.name: Shield Block
action->Shield Block.description: If the umbral gnome warrior has their shield raised, they can spend your reaction to block a physical attack. Reduce the amount of damage by 5, but then the umbral gnome warrior and their shield both take any damage leftover. This might break or destroy the shield. The shield has a maximum of 20 healing Hit Points.

Viper (BB).name: Viper (EB)
Viper (BB).publicNotes:
Jeder Angehörige dieser Familie von Giftschlangen besitzt lange, einklappbare Zähne, welche der Beute ein kraftvolles Gift injizieren. Unterschiedliche Arten haben natürlich auch unterschiedliche Gift, so dass es Viperngifte gibt, welche Lähmung hervorrufen, sehr schmerzhafte Schwellungen, Gerinnung des Blutes oder sogar einen plötzlichen Herzstillstand.

Die gemeine Viper ist zwar weniger beeindruckend als eine Riesenviper, aber dennoch gefährlicher, als ihre Größe vermuten ließe. Vipern jagen hauptsächlich kleine Säugetiere und Eidechsen, greifen aber auch Humanoide an, von denen sie sich bedroht fühlen.

action->Slink.name: Fortschlängeln
action->Slink.description: Beendet eine Kreatur ihre Bewegung angrenzend zur Viper oder innerhalb ihrer Angriffsfläche, kann die Viper ihre Reaktion nutzen, um sich mittels Laufen, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.pprgrYQ1QnIDGZiy]{Klettern} oder @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.c8TGiZ48ygoSPofx]{Schwimmen} bis zu 10 Fuß/3 m (oder weniger, sollte die fragliche Bewegungsrate keine 10 Fuß/3 m erlauben) fortzubewegen. Sie muss ihre Bewegung an einem Ort beenden, der mehr als 5 Fuß/1,50 m von einem Gegner entfernt ist. Diese Bewegung löst keine Reaktionen aus.

action->Viper Venom.name: Viperngift
action->Viper Venom.description: Erleidet ein Ziel durch die Viper Bissschaden, muss es einen @Check[fortitude|dc:16]{Zähigkeitswurf} ablegen; bei Misslingen erleidet es @Damage[1d10[poison]]{1W10 Punkte Giftschaden}.

Warg (BB).name: Warg (EB)
Warg (BB).publicNotes: Warge werden oft mit Wölfen verwechselt, aber sie verfügen über eine listige Intelligenz und die Fähigkeit zu sprechen. Warge bilden oft Jagdgruppen mit Orks und Hobgoblins. Die meisten Warge sind offen für derartige Absprachen, sollte das Essen allerdings knapp werden, sind Warge mehr als nur bereit, auch goblinoides Fleisch auf die Speisekarte zu setzen.

action->Avenging Bite.name: Rächender Biss
action->Avenging Bite.description: Greift eine Kreatur innerhalb des Angriffsradius des Bissangriffs des Wargs einen Verbündeten des Wargs an, dann kann der Warg seine Reaktion nutzen und einen Bissangriff gegen diese Kreatur ausführen.

action->Pack Attack.name: Rudelangriff
action->Pack Attack.description: Der Bissangriff des Wargs fügt jeder Kreatur zusätzliche 1W4 Schadenspunkte zu, die sich im Angriffsradius von wenigstens zweien seiner Verbündeten befindet.

action->Shred.name: Zerfetzen
action->Shred.description: If the warg has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed}, they can spend one action to shred it with their teeth. The creature takes @Damage[1d6[slashing]] damage and can attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:18|basic] save.

action->Grab.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.Tkd8sH4pwFIPzqTr]

When the warg succeeds at its jaws Strike, it can use this action immediately after to attempt to [[/act grapple]] the target. This Grapple doesn't apply or count toward the warg's multiple attack penalty.

Water Scamp (BB).name: Water Scamp (BB)
Water Scamp (BB).publicNotes: Elemental scamps are bat-like critters marked by elemental powers. Water scamps are marked apart from other scamps by sleek fur that traps a layer of water next to their skin. Although they can fly like their kin and conjure enough water to never dry out, water scamps leave the water only when they must.

spell->Acid Grip.name: Acid Grip
spell->Acid Grip.description:
An ephemeral, taloned hand grips the target, burning it with magical acid. The target takes 2d8 acid damage plus @Damage[floor(@item.level/2)d6[persistent,acid]] damage depending on its Reflex save. A creature taking persistent damage from this spell takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spell-effects.Item.6TGcfVyzzVHEo7ke]{Spell Effect: Acid Grip}

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature takes half damage and no persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 5 feet in a direction of your choice.

Failure The creature takes full damage and persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 10 feet in a direction of your choice.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage and full persistent damage, and the claw moves it up to 20 feet in a direction of your choice.

Heightened (+2) The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the persistent acid damage increases by 1d6.

action->Acid Breath.name: Acid Breath
action->Acid Breath.description: The water scamp breathes acid that deals @Damage[2d6[acid]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15]. Each creature in the area must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:17|basic|options:area-effect] save. The water scamp can't use Acid Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Acid Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Drench.name: Drench
action->Drench.description: The water scamp shakes out a seemingly endless supply of water from its fur to put out all non-magical fires within 5 feet.

Web Lurker (BB).name: Netzlaurer (EB)
Web Lurker (BB).publicNotes: Netzlaurer werden zuweilen auch als Atterkoppe bezeichnet. Diese spinnenartigen Monster hausen in ihren Verstecken mit Spinnen und Schwärmen und kultivieren solches Ungeziefer aktiv. Netzlaurer jagen in Wäldern, Sümpfen und mit Buschwerk bewachsenen Hügellanden. Sie errichten ihre Nester aus ihren Netzen hoch in den Baumwipfeln oder in zerklüfteten Schluchten und umgeben sie mit Fallen aus Netzfäden und natürlichen Materialien.

action->Spider Speak.name: Mit Spinnen sprechen
action->Spider Speak.description: Der Netzlaurer kann mit Spinnen sprechen und diese verstehen.

action->Web Lurker Venom.name: Netzlaurergift
action->Web Lurker Venom.description: Erleidet ein Ziel durch die Zähne des Netzlaurers Schaden, muss es einen @Check[fortitude|dc:19]{Zähigkeitswurf} ablegen; bei Misslingen erhält es @Damage[1d12[poison]]{1W12 Punkte Giftschaden} und für 1 Runde den Zustand @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.AJh5ex99aV6VTggg]{Auf dem Falschen Fuß}.

action->Web Trap.name: Netzfalle
action->Web Trap.description: Eine vom Spinnennetzangriff getroffene Kreatur klebt an der nächsten Oberfläche und kann sich nicht fortbewegen ([[/act escape dc=20]]).

Wight (BB).name: Gruftschrecken (EB)
Wight (BB).publicNotes: Gruftschrecken sind untote Humanoide, die in Friedhöfen, Katakomben oder anderen Stätten der Toten lauern.

action->Final Spite.name: Trotziger Schlag
action->Final Spite.description: When the wight is reduced to 0 Hit Points, they can use their reaction to make a Strike before being destroyed. They don't gain any Hit Points from corrupting spite on this Strike.

action->Corrupting Spite.name: Verderbender Hass
action->Corrupting Spite.description: When the wight deals damage to a living creature with their claw or dagger Strike, the wight regains @Damage[2[healing]] Hit Points and the target must succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:17|options:damaging-effect] save or take @Damage[2] damage that can't be healed. The wight can use corrupting spite multiple times, but a single creature can't take more than 8 unhealable damage. Each full night of rest heals 2 of the unhealable damage, and a full day of bed rest increases the recovery of the unhealable damage to 4.

Wolf (BB).name: Wolf (EB)
Wolf (BB).publicNotes: Wolves roam forests, hills, and other wild lands where they live and hunt in packs. Contrary to popular belief, these packs aren't led by the strongest in the group, but typically consist of a mated pair, their pups, and juvenile offspring from previous mating seasons. Offspring generally leave their parents' pack upon reaching maturity, at which point they seek out mates of their own to form their own packs elsewhere. Wolves don't typically view humanoids as prey animals, but extraordinary circumstances can lead wolves to attack people, especially in winter months and other situations where traditional prey (such as deer and elk) are scarce.

action->Pack Attack.name: Rudelangriff
action->Pack Attack.description: Der Bissangriff des Wolfs fügt jeder Kreatur zusätzliche 1W4 Schadenspunkte zu, die sich im Angriffsradius von wenigstens zweien seiner Verbündeten befindet.

action->Knockdown.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.BCLvAx4Pz4MLa2pu]

When the wolf succeeds at its jaws Strike, it can use this action immediately after to attempt to [[/act trip]] the target. This Trip doesn't apply or count toward the wolf's multiple attack penalty.

Xulgath Leader (BB).name: Xulgathanführer (EB)
Xulgath Leader (BB).publicNotes: Xulgathanführer sind stärker und neigen eher zu Gewalt als die anderen Mitglieder eines Xulgathenstammes.

action->Stench.name: Gestank
action->Stench.description: A creature that comes @Template[emanation|distance:30]{within 30} feet of the xulgath and can smell the xulgath must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:19|options:inflicts:sickened] save. On a failure, the creature takes a –1 status penalty to all checks and DCs until it uses an action to retch (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}). A creature that succeeds at its save isn't affected by any xulgaths' stenches for 1 minute.

action->Swipe.name: Durchschlagender Angriff
action->Swipe.description: The xulgath leader makes two Strikes with their greataxe against two adjacent foes, both of whom are within their reach, with a +1 circumstance bonus. The second attack uses the same multiple attack penalty as the first, but attacks the xulgath boss makes after this take a –10 multiple attack penalty (or a –8 multiple attack penalty for its claw).

Xulgath Warrior (BB).name: Xulgathkrieger (EB)
Xulgath Warrior (BB).publicNotes: Die vielen Oberflächenbewohnern auch als Troglodyten bekannten Xulgathen greifen jeden Eindringling an, der ihr Revier betritt. Sie leben in Familienverbänden und bekämpfen andere Sippen und andere Bewohner der Tiefe, um zu überleben. Zuweilen überfallen sie angetrieben von grausamen und blutdurstigen Anführern auch Ortschaften an der Oberfläche. Ein typischer Xulgath hat stumpf-, dunkel- oder aschegraue Schuppen und einen langen Schwanz. Entlang dem Rückgrat wachsen knöcherne Auswüchse.

action->Stench.name: Gestank
action->Stench.description: A creature that comes within 30 feet of the xulgath and can smell the xulgath must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:16|options:inflicts:sickened] save. On a failure, the creature takes a –1 status penalty to all checks and DCs until it uses an action to retch (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}). A creature that succeeds at its save isn't affected by any xulgaths' stenches for 1 minute.

Zephyr Hawk (BB).name: Windfalke (EB)
Zephyr Hawk (BB).publicNotes:
Luftelementare wie den Windfalken gibt es in unterschiedlichen Größen und Formen. Windfalken werden oft als fliegende Boten von der Ebene der Luft herbeibeschworen, können aber auch spontan während heftiger Stürme und Tornados auf die Materielle Ebene überwechseln, wenn sich elementare Wirbel bilden.

Windfalken scheinen aus Wolken zu bestehen. Sieht jemand vogelartige Wolken am Himmel ziehen, beobachtet er oft ahnungslos Windfalken. Windfalken lassen sich zuweilen in großen Schwärmen auf Luftströmungen treiben.

action->Circling Attack.name: Kreisender Angriff
action->Circling Attack.description: Der Windfalke nutzt Fliegen, um sich mit maximal seiner halben Bewegungsrate für Fliegen zu bewegen, führt dann zwei Flügelangriffe aus und nutzt noch einmal Fliegen, um sich mit maximal seiner halben Bewegungsrate für Fliegen zu bewegen und so an seinen Ausgangspunkt zurückzukehren. Die zweite Hälfte der Fortbewegung löst keine Reaktionen wie z.B. Reaktionsangriff aus. Beide Angriffe zählen gegen den Malus für Mehrfachangriffe des Windfalken und unterliegen demselben Malus für Mehrfachangriffe; der Malus steigt aber erst nach Ausführung der beiden Angriffe auf -8 an.

Zombie Shambler (BB).name: Zombieschlurfer (EB)
Zombie Shambler (BB).publicNotes: Das einzige Verlangen eines Zombies ist es, alles Lebende zu verzehren. Die unaufhaltsam torkelnden und zum Denken unfähigen Boten des Todes lassen sich nur aufhalten, wenn sie vollständig zerstört werden. Untote nutzen Zombies oft als Kanonenfutter, welches Verteidigungen schwächt und niederwalzt, bis mächtigere Untote eintreffen und den Todesstoß liefern. Zombies können nicht wirklich selbständig denken, sie können aber von intelligenten Untoten und mächtigen Nekromanten befehligt werden.

strike-melee->Jaws.name: Biss
strike-melee->Jaws.description: If the zombie has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}, it can attempt this special Strike against the grabbed or restrained creature.

action->Slow.name: Langsam
action->Slow.description: Ein Zombie besitzt nur 2 Aktionen während seines Zuges und kann keine Reaktionen nutzen.

action->Grab.description:
tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-ability-glossary-srd.Item.Tkd8sH4pwFIPzqTr]

When the zombie succeeds at its fist Strike, it can use this action immediately after to attempt to [[/act grapple]] the target. This Grapple doesn't apply or count toward the zombie's multiple attack penalty.
